title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: RcppOpenMP R package provides example parallelizing C++ routine OpenMP directly inside Rcpp function. pure-C++ companion rcpp-c--openmp, instead parallelizes separate C routine.

title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: install package, must first compiler system compatible R supports OpenMP. help obtaining compiler consult either macOS Windows guides. compiler hand, one can install package GitHub :

title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: goal project show single Rcpp function can parallelized OpenMP without leaving C++. two moving parts: Rcpp function carries OpenMP directive, Makevars files turn OpenMP portable way. example routine computes Euclidean norm row numeric matrix. work row independent, loop rows natural candidate parallelization.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── RcppExports.R                   # Autogenerated R to C++ bindings by Rcpp │   └── RcppOpenMP-package.R            # Package documentation ├── README.md ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── RcppOpenMP-package.Rd │   └── parallel_row_norms.Rd └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── Makevars                        # Enable OpenMP on Unix-alikes (macOS/Linux)     ├── Makevars.win                    # Enable OpenMP on Windows     ├── RcppExports.cpp                 # Autogenerated R bindings     └── parallel_row_norms.cpp          # The OpenMP-parallelized Rcpp routine

title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: routine lives src/parallel_row_norms.cpp. <omp.h> header included compiler advertises OpenMP support _OPENMP macro, keeps routine compiling cleanly toolchains lack OpenMP. #pragma omp parallel directive splits outer loop rows across available threads. exported function also carries //' roxygen comments (trimmed excerpt ); Rcpp::compileAttributes() copies R/RcppExports.R, package’s R help page generated .
code: #include <Rcpp.h> #include <cmath>  #ifdef _OPENMP #include <omp.h> #endif  // [[Rcpp::export]] Rcpp::NumericVector parallel_row_norms(Rcpp::NumericMatrix x) {   int n = x.nrow();   int p = x.ncol();   Rcpp::NumericVector out(n);    #pragma omp parallel for   for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {     double ss = 0.0;     for (int j = 0; j < p; j++) {       double v = x(i, j);       ss += v * v;     }     out[i] = std::sqrt(ss);   }    return out; }

title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: OpenMP enabled src/Makevars (Unix-alikes) src/Makevars.win (Windows). Rather hard-coding flag -fopenmp, files use R’s $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CXXFLAGS) variable, expands correct OpenMP flag whichever C++ compiler R built . variable supplied PKG_LIBS OpenMP runtime linked . parallel #pragma lives .cpp file, C++ flag $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CXXFLAGS) correct sufficient choice . rcpp-c--openmp example places directive .c file instead, additionally sets matching PKG_CFLAGS = $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CFLAGS) translation unit alongside C++ flag.

title: Using OpenMP with Rcpp
text: body parallel loop reads input matrix writes pre-allocated output vector, thread writing distinct index. never allocates Rcpp object calls R API, keeps routine thread-safe. Touching R API inside OpenMP region, example creating Rcpp::NumericVector calling R function, safe, R single-threaded. Allocate R objects parallel region, output vector allocated , confine loop body plain C++. number threads governed OpenMP runtime, example OMP_NUM_THREADS environment variable, function .

title: Row Euclidean Norms Computed in Parallel with OpenMP — parallel_row_norms
text: #pragma omp parallel directive splits outer loop rows across available OpenMP threads. directive meaningful package compiled OpenMP support; without loop simply runs serially. input matrix read pre-allocated output vector written inside parallel region, thread writes distinct index. R API touched inside loop, keeps routine thread-safe.
